mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.24% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevated FEDFUNDS above 5.0% signals continued tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1850K units signal robust residential construction demand. Sustained housing activity above 1800K units indicates strong household formation and consumer confidence, driving positive equity returns through increased corporate earnings from construction and related sectors over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1850K units as of June 2006
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong housing demand
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods when HOUST > 1800K, or housing-driven earnings growth fails to materialize (construction sector earnings growth < 2% annualized).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.8% combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 135.354M represents tight labor market conditions. When UNRATE remains below 5.0% and PAYEMS shows month-over-month growth, this signals strong labor demand that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% and PAYEMS at 135.354M as of July 2006
regime_hint: full employment expansion with wage pressure
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 52% of instances when both UNRATE < 5.0% and PAYEMS growth > 0%, or consumer spending growth decouples from labor market tightness (correlation < 0.3 over 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 113.21 reflects manufacturing capacity utilization. A sustained decline in INDPRO below its 3-month moving average signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices cyclical earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of instances when INDPRO declines below 3-month MA, or manufacturing earnings growth remains positive despite INDPRO weakness (correlation < 0.25).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 367.941B and real GDP (GDPC1) at 11385.3B indicate aggregate demand strength. When RSAFS growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 5.0%, this signals demand destruction from monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 367.941B and FEDFUNDS at 5.24% as of July 2006
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both RSAFS growth < 0.2% and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or retail earnings fail to decline despite sales weakness (correlation < 0.35).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 202.3 combined with FEDFUNDS at 5.24% indicates the Fed is in active tightening mode with real rates above neutral. If CPIAUCSL growth accelerates above 3% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%, the Fed will maintain restrictive policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real discount rates remain elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 202.3 and FEDFUNDS at 5.24% as of July 2006
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.0% and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Fed rate cuts occur despite inflation readings (policy reversal invalidates mechanism).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1850K combined with retail sales (RSAFS) at 367.941B represent dual demand strength. When both HOUST and RSAFS show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this signals broad-based consumer and construction demand that sustains corporate earnings across multiple sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1850K and RSAFS at 367.941B as of June-July 2006
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 52% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS show positive growth, or earnings growth fails to materialize despite dual demand signals (correlation < 0.30).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.8% represents tight labor market. When UNRATE rises above 5.0% in a single month, this signals an inflection point in the employment cycle that precedes broader economic slowdown. Rising unemployment triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% in month-over-month change
regime_hint: employment cycle inflection from full employment to softening
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or unemployment rises without subsequent earnings deterioration (correlation < 0.25 over 2-year window).
